---
title: "Gutsy no longer automounter my ntfs drives or partions"
date: 2007-11-09
forum: Absolute Beginner Talk
---

### Post by Bogdon6 on 2007-11-09
Gutsy has been great, but today I turned on my computer and Gutsy is no longer automouting my NTFS hard drives.  I read in the comments that I needed to make sure that the drives were in the /etc/fstab file and they are.  Here's the file:

> # /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/hda2 :
UUID=8e4b369a-5f3c-4dca-800a-e850da92032a / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/hda5 :
UUID=6fc5ffc8-295e-42b1-b7f3-50a6b8a78c7e none swap sw 0 0
/dev/cdrom /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/hdd /media/cdrom1 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/ /media/floppy0 auto rw,user,noauto 0 0
/dev/hda1 /media/hda1 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.utf8 0 0
/dev/hdb1 /media/hdb1 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.utf8 0 0
/dev/sda1 /media/sda1 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.utf8 0 0
What should I do so that Gutsy starts automounting my drives again?

---

### Post by Bogdon6 on 2007-11-10
I believe I figured out what the problem was--Windows XP failing to shut down properly. My computer dual-boots with Gutsy and XP. The last time I used XP, the computer failed to shut down properly. I started up XP and then exited properly. Now Gutsy recognizes the drives automatically.

---
